competition: The South African Mathematical Olympiad Third Round
topics_flat: Discrete Mathematics > Combinatorics > Games / greedy algorithms
prompt:
Andile and Zandre play a game on a $2017 \times 2017$ board. At the beginning, Andile declares some of the squares *forbidden*, meaning that nothing may be placed on such a square. After that, they take turns to place coins on the board, with Zandre placing the first coin. It is not allowed to place a coin on a forbidden square or in the same row or column where another coin has already been placed. The player who places the last coin wins the game.
What is the least number of squares Andile needs to declare as forbidden at the beginning to ensure a win? (Assume that both players use an optimal strategy.)
response_text:
Let \(n=2017\). After some squares are forbidden, the game is exactly this:

- A move consists of choosing an **allowed** square \((r,c)\),
- after which row \(r\) and column \(c\) can never be used again.

So the game is the same as building a matching in the bipartite graph whose left vertices are the rows, right vertices are the columns, and whose edges are the allowed squares. The game ends when the matching is maximal, and the number of moves played is the size of the matching produced.

Since the winner is determined only by the parity of the total number of moves, Andile wants to force **every** maximal play to have even length.

We show the minimum number of forbidden squares needed is \(2017\).

## 1. A construction with \(2017\) forbidden squares

Forbid all diagonal squares
\[
(1,1),(2,2),\dots,(2017,2017).
\]
So the allowed graph is \(K_{2017,2017}\) with a perfect matching removed.

Claim: every maximal matching has size \(2016\).

Indeed, Hall’s condition shows there is no matching of size \(2017\): if we take all rows, their neighbors are all columns except possibly the “corresponding” one, so a perfect matching is impossible because this graph is exactly \(K_{n,n}\setminus M\), where \(M\) is a perfect matching; equivalently, the complement of allowed edges is a perfect matching. But there is clearly a matching of size \(2016\), e.g.
\[
(1,2),(2,3),\dots,(2016,2017).
\]
Hence the maximum matching size is \(2016\), and any maximal play ends after \(2016\) moves, which is even. Therefore Zandre (who starts) loses, so Andile wins.

Thus \(2017\) forbidden squares are **sufficient**.

---

## 2. Fewer than \(2017\) forbidden squares can never suffice

Suppose Andile forbids only \(m<2017\) squares.

Consider the forbidden squares as edges of a bipartite graph \(F\) between rows and columns. Since \(F\) has fewer than \(2017\) edges, its connected components are very sparse. In particular, \(F\) cannot contain a cycle spanning all \(2017\) rows/columns; more importantly, by König’s theorem / Hall’s theorem, there exists a matching in the **allowed** graph of size \(2017\).

A simpler way to see this is via Hall:

For any set \(S\) of rows, let \(N(S)\) be the columns containing at least one allowed square in those rows. If Hall failed, then for some \(S\),
\[
|N(S)|<|S|.
\]
That means every allowed square in those rows lies inside fewer than \(|S|\) columns, so every square from rows \(S\) to the other \(2017-|N(S)|\) columns must be forbidden. Hence the number of forbidden squares is at least
\[
|S|\bigl(2017-|N(S)|\bigr)\ge |S|(2017-(|S|-1))=|S|(2018-|S|).
\]
For \(1\le |S|\le 2017\), this quantity is at least \(2017\), contradiction.

So Hall’s condition holds, and there is a perfect matching of size \(2017\) in the allowed graph.

Now Zandre can simply play according to some perfect matching: on each turn, choose an unused edge from that perfect matching. This is always possible until all \(2017\) matching edges are used. Therefore the game lasts at least \(2017\) moves, and in fact exactly \(2017\) moves since no more than one coin can be placed in any row or column.

Thus with fewer than \(2017\) forbidden squares, Zandre can force an odd number of moves and hence win.

So \(2017\) forbidden squares are **necessary**.

Therefore the least number Andile needs is

Final answer: \(2017\)
